setting.note: A country house in the Surrey heat of 1935, where a girl's misreading of one scene wrecks three lives, then the war that follows it: a soldier's long walk to the beaches of Dunkirk, and the London wards where the two sisters nurse the wounded. The map anchors on southern England, from the Surrey estate to wartime Lambeth and Balham, and reaches across the Channel to the French coast of the retreat.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Ian McEwan, first published 2001. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Atonement, Ian McEwan (2001). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: A cool grey-blue for the water that runs through the book, from the fountain on the Surrey lawn to the Channel at Dunkirk. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
